accepted.reason: Dominance comparison logic is a compact multi-objective ranking kernel suitable for optimization tasks.
Gaussian weighted averaging and channel resampling are clean numerical kernels with repeated vector operations.
Route splitting dynamic-programming logic with capacity/length constraints is a strong combinatorial optimization example.
Character-level state-machine validation for email addresses is a practical parsing kernel.
Escape-time Mandelbrot iteration is a classic iterative numeric benchmark with branch-heavy control flow.
Row proxy key-resolution and accessor methods are high-value structured-data access patterns.
Fast geometric/retinal model kernels and nearest-segment lookup provide useful math-heavy examples.

skipped.reason: Cython runtime/fastcall behavior test rather than a standalone optimization kernel.
Tightly coupled to project-specific class state and external helpers, reducing standalone portability.
CPython C-API regression tests are compiler/runtime checks, not reusable conversion targets.
